findings.title: excludes_openrouter ignores new OpenRouter provider prefixes
findings.evidence: is_openrouter_routed() only returns True for ('deepseek/', 'x-ai/'), while get_builder routes z-ai/, qwen/, moonshotai/, minimax/, xiaomi/ to OpenRouter.
findings.impact: A risk profile marked excludes_openrouter can still select newly added OR-bound voices, causing disqualified Phase 2 outputs at best and policy drift across Phase 1/Phase 4.
findings.poc: filter_candidates(['z-ai/glm-4.6', 'qwen/qwen3.6-max-preview', 'mistral/devstral-2'], RiskProfile(excludes_openrouter=True)) keeps z-ai and qwen, although src/polybuild/adapters/__init__.py:87-96 routes them to OpenRouter.

findings.title: Remote qwen/* is treated as local Qwen under US/CN exclusion
findings.evidence: The qwen branch returns not voice_id.startswith('qwen'), so both qwen2.5-coder local and qwen/qwen3.6 remote are treated as non-US/CN.
findings.impact: A profile with excludes_us_cn_models=True but excludes_openrouter=False can retain Alibaba-hosted qwen/* via OpenRouter, violating the privacy routing contract.
findings.poc: filter_candidates(['qwen/qwen3.6-max-preview'], RiskProfile(excludes_us_cn_models=True)) keeps the remote qwen voice; config/models.yaml:174-186 documents qwen/qwen3.6-* as OpenRouter optional.
findings.remediation:
- if voice_id.startswith(("kimi-", "deepseek/", "qwen")):
-     return not voice_id.startswith("qwen")
+ if voice_id.startswith("qwen2.5-coder:"):
+     return False
+ if voice_id.startswith(("kimi-", "deepseek/", "qwen/")):
+     return True

findings.title: New Chinese voices are in routing but absent from model_dimensions
findings.evidence: routing.yaml adds z-ai/qwen/moonshotai in cross_cultural_diversity at lines 273-289, but model_dimensions OpenRouter entries stop at deepseek and x-ai.
findings.impact: Phase 1 can assign family='unknown' for selected new voices; if such a voice wins, select_auditor() cannot find winner_family and the audit path can abort after generation.
findings.poc: select_voices() uses cfg['dimensions'].get(v, {}).get('provider', 'unknown') at src/polybuild/phases/phase_1_select.py:222-226; select_auditor() raises for unknown winner family at lines 259-268.
findings.remediation:
+ z-ai/glm-4.6:
+   provider: zai
+   ...
+ qwen/qwen3.6-max-preview:
+   provider: qwen
+   ...
+ add auditor_pools_by_winner_family entries for new providers

findings.title: Polybuild skill interpolates unescaped args into tmux shell command
findings.evidence: SPEC is parsed from user args, EXTRA is built with '${EXTRA_ARGS[*]}', then both are interpolated into a quoted tmux command at lines 45-50.
findings.impact: A malicious spec path or extra arg containing a quote or shell metacharacter can execute arbitrary shell commands in the developer session.
findings.poc: /polybuild run --spec "brief.md'; touch .polybuild/PWNED; #" reaches: uv run polybuild run --spec '${SPEC}' ... inside the tmux shell string.
findings.remediation:
+ use a generated wrapper script with an argv array
+ printf '%q' for SPEC and every EXTRA_ARGS item before embedding
- EXTRA="${EXTRA_ARGS[*]}"

findings.title: Malformed CLI JSON is normalized as Status.OK
findings.evidence: _try_parse_json(raw) or {} falls through to BuilderResult(status=Status.OK) even when no JSON payload was parsed.
findings.impact: Adapter/CLI contract failures are reported as successful builder results with empty worktrees, pushing failure detection downstream into scoring instead of failing at the boundary.
findings.poc: tests/regression/test_cli_file_write_parsing.py:113-124 asserts malformed raw output returns Status.OK and writes zero files.
findings.remediation:
+ data = _try_parse_json(raw)
+ if data is None:
+     return self._failed_result(cfg, worktree, duration, 'Invalid JSON output')
- data = _try_parse_json(raw) or {}

findings.title: Phase 3 gate coroutines are awaited sequentially, not in parallel
findings.evidence: The code creates coroutine objects, then awaits pytest_task, mypy_task, ruff_task, bandit_task, gitleaks_task one after another.
findings.impact: The slowest gates do not overlap; prod-launch scoring latency is roughly the sum of gate durations despite the comment claiming parallel execution.
findings.poc: Because run_command() is an async function, calling it without create_task does not start execution. First subprocess starts only at await pytest_task on line 102.
findings.remediation:
+ tasks = [asyncio.create_task(run_command(...)), ...]
+ results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
- _pytest_rc, pytest_out, pytest_err = await pytest_task

findings.title: Path traversal regression assertions are neutered with 'or True'
findings.evidence: The absolute path tests end with assert not Path('/tmp/evil').exists() or True and assert not Path('/etc/passwd').exists() or True.
findings.impact: A future regression that writes to the forbidden absolute path could still pass this assertion, weakening coverage for the sprint's main P0 security fix.
findings.poc: Any boolean expression followed by 'or True' always passes; lines 57 and tests/regression/test_cli_file_write_parsing.py:283 cannot detect filesystem writes.
findings.remediation:
+ outside = tmp_path / 'outside.py'
+ n = write_files_to_worktree({str(outside): 'x'}, worktree)
+ assert n == 0
+ assert not outside.exists()

findings.title: Production runbook points to stale commit and wrong checkout path
findings.evidence: The runbook states commit e7f3d86 at line 3 and uses /Users/radu/Downloads/polybuild_v3-2 at lines 22 and 57, while the audited target is 2b3c12f under /Users/radu/Developer/projects/polybuild_v3.
findings.impact: Operators following the runbook can install and smoke-test a stale tree, invalidating the prod-launch validation for this sprint.
findings.poc: Running the pre-flight exactly as documented installs from /Users/radu/Downloads/polybuild_v3-2, not the audited local repository.
findings.remediation:
- État au commit `e7f3d86`
+ État au commit `2b3c12f`
- cd /Users/radu/Downloads/polybuild_v3-2
+ cd /Users/radu/Developer/projects/polybuild_v3

findings.title: Dry-run runs are reported as committed even when Phase 7 is skipped
findings.evidence: PolybuildRun is initialized with final_status='committed' before the skip_commit branch; if skip_commit is True, commit_sha remains None but status stays committed.
findings.impact: The documented --no-commit smoke/low-risk runs can return a misleading committed status, corrupting postmortem data and CLI success semantics.
findings.poc: Run with --no-commit --no-smoke: lines 723-729 skip phase_7_commit(), but no line changes final_status away from 'committed'.
findings.remediation:
+ final_status='validated' if skip_commit else 'committed'
+ if not skip_commit:
+     ...
+     run.final_status = 'committed'

false_positives_avoided: H1 and H2 were searched and not reported as real symbols.
I did not report residual traversal in safe_write: it resolves the target and enforces is_relative_to(worktree.resolve()).
I did not claim confirmed Phase 2 PII exfiltration for excludes_openrouter because OpenRouterAdapter.generate has its own disqualification guard.
I did not report O(n^2) regex behavior in _json_extract; the visible regex is lazy and no quadratic PoC was established.
I did not rerun the full 199-test suite in this read-only audit; findings are based on code and test inspection.
